name: Salmon Burgers with Pineapple Rings
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons firmly packed light brown sugar
1/2 teaspoon Oriental sesame oil (or 1/2 teaspoon vegetable oil)
recipeInstructions:
Burgers
1 1/4 pounds skinless salmon fillet, cut into 2-inch pieces
2 tablespoons green onion, chopped
2 cloves garlic, finely chopped
1 inch piece fresh gingerroot, peeled and grated
2 teaspoons reduced-sodium soy sauce
1 teaspoon Oriental sesame oil
4 3-1/2 inch thick fresh pineapple rings
6 small green onions, cut lengthwise into halves and ends trimmed
4 sesame-seed rolls, split
1. Several hours or a day before serving, prepare Teriyaki Marinade: In a small jar with a tight-fitting lid, whisk together soy sauce, sugar, lemon juice, sesame oil, and ginger; cover and set aside until ready to use.
2. Several hours before serving, prepare salmon mixture for Burgers: In a food processor fitted with a chopping blade, process salmon, green onion, garlic, gingerroot, soy sauce, and sesame oil until well combined. Divide into 4 equal portions; shape each into a 5-inch round burger. Cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour before grilling (Burgers must be refrigerated for the recommended time to maintain shape during cooking).
3. About 30 minutes before grilling, heat indoor or outdoor grill to medium. In a medium-sized bowl, combine pineapple rings, green onions, and Teriyaki Marinade; cover and refrigerate for 10 minutes.
4. Drain marinade into serving pitcher and set aside.
5. Coat grill rack with nonstick cooking spray. Grill burgers 6 inches above heat until cooked through, turning once, about 10 minutes. Grill pineapple rings and green onions until heated through, turning once, about 1 to 2 minutes.
6. On 4 individual plates, assemble burgers: Place salmon burgers on bottom halves of rolls. Top each with a pineapple slice and 3 green onion halves; cover with tops of rolls. Serve with reserved Teriyaki Marinade.
